page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Eav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs and is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type does not include local storage, meaning it relies entirely on external storage options, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. It does not feature any onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the E96as_v6 offers a high allocation of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial RAM without the overhead of GPU hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for demanding enterprise applications. This configuration is suitable for large-scale databases, memory-intensive analytics, and enterprise business workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity. Powered by an AMD x86_64 processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU. The server does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. It contains no onboard GPUs and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its high memory density and dedicated compute resources, the E96as_v6 is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, including large relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise-grade data processing workloads.
